headlineSummary: Sentiment is mostly neutral overall, with OpenAI, Anthropic, and Microsoft Copilot in a close tie on limited evidence, while Gemini gets a mixed read: praised as useful but also described as needing mentoring and making conceptual errors [E1] [E2] [E3].

ranking.summary: OpenAI’s tone is mixed-neutral on limited evidence: one comment praises ChatGPT 5.5 Pro as capable of producing a publishable paper, while another calls 1-800 ChatGPT “trash now” and says it keeps interrupting [E1] [E3].
Anthropic is mixed-neutral on limited evidence, driven by a complaint that Claude Opus was recently denied access [E2].
Microsoft Copilot is mixed-neutral on limited evidence because the comment describes Microsoft’s Copilot policy as a “clampdown” on individual and academic use [E2].

Google Gemini is mixed-neutral on limited evidence: one commenter calls it a formidable tool for catching errors and spotting connections, but also says it often makes conceptual mistakes and still needs a lot of mentoring [E1].

evidence.excerpt: I am a physics professor and often use Gemini to check my papers. It is a formidable tool: it was able to find a clerical error (a missing imaginary unit in a complex mathematical expression) I was not able to find for days, and it often underlines connections between concepts and ideas that I overlooked. However, it often makes conceptual errors that I can spot only because I have good knowledge of the topic I am discussing. For instance, in 3D Clifford algebras it repeatedly confuses exponential of bivectors and of pseudoscalars. Good to know that ChatGPT 5.5 Pro can produce a publishable paper, but from what I have seen so far with Gemini, it seems to me that it is better to consider LLMs as very efficient students who can read papers and books in no time but still need a lot of mentoring.

annotations.rationale: The comment praises Gemini as 'a formidable tool' that found a real error, but also says it 'often makes conceptual errors' and still needs 'a lot of mentoring'.
The commenter says 'ChatGPT 5.5 Pro can produce a publishable paper,' which is mild praise for capability.

evidence.excerpt: As a nail to the coffin, I was "denied" all Claude Opus recently as part of Microsoft's clampdown on individual (and academic) use of Copilot.

annotations.rationale: The commenter complains about being denied access to Claude Opus.
Describes Microsoft's Copilot policy as a 'clampdown' on individual and academic use.

evidence.excerpt: Oh is this why 1 800 CHAT GPT is trash now? It worked great when I started using it months ago. Last few times I've called the bot constantly interrupts herself, or stops as if I'm interrupting. I can't get a single full sentence out of her so I stopped calling.

annotations.rationale: The commenter calls 1-800 ChatGPT 'trash now' and says it constantly interrupts and is unusable.
